name: Dining Cryptographers
description: Three cryptographers dine together. The waiter announces the bill has been paid anonymously. Determine whether one of you paid — or someone else did.
prompt:
You are one of three cryptographers at a dinner. The waiter has just announced that the bill has been paid — but by whom?

At the start you will be told privately whether you paid the bill. Your goal is to figure out whether the payer was one of the diners or an external party.

You may also try to identify exactly who among the diners paid.

Send your guess using the `guess` method:
- `external` — you think an outside party paid
- `internal` — you think one of the diners paid, but you're not naming anyone
- `player:0`, `player:1`, `player:2` — you think a specific diner (by index) paid

Scoring:
- **Utility +1** if you correctly guess the category (external vs. a diner)
- **Utility -1** if you guess the wrong category
- **Security**: if a diner paid and every player names that specific diner, the payer's security score is -1. Otherwise the payer's security is +1.

methods.name: guess
methods.description: Submit your guess. Options: `external`, `internal`, or `player:N` (where N is a player index, e.g. `player:0`).
